insight: The hydration breaks in the 2026 FIFA World Cup have had a notable impact on the United States team, with the team gaining momentum in both of their breaks, resulting in a mean increase of 0.150 points per minute. This suggests that the breaks have been beneficial for the team, allowing them to regroup and come back stronger. The data also shows that when the United States was not pressing before a break, they tended to gain more momentum afterwards, with an average increase of 0.300 points per minute, indicating that the break gave them an opportunity to recharge and come back with renewed energy. Overall, the hydration breaks seem to be a strategic advantage for the United States, allowing them to shift the momentum of the game in their favor.
